nodes.title: Reject non-string input early
nodes.why: It keeps the rest of the checks safe and avoids unexpected runtime behavior.
nodes.alternative: Coerce any input with `String(name)` before validating.
nodes.tradeoff: Stricter and predictable, but less forgiving for callers.

nodes.title: Enforce minimum and maximum length
nodes.why: Length rules are cheap to check and quickly rule out invalid usernames.
nodes.alternative: Put length logic inside one regex pattern.
nodes.tradeoff: Clear and easy to test, with one extra explicit step.

nodes.title: Use pattern + edge-case guards
nodes.why: The regex handles allowed characters and start-letter rule, while later checks catch underscore edge cases.
nodes.alternative: Use one complex regex for everything.
nodes.tradeoff: More readable and maintainable, though slightly more lines of code.

nodes.title: Gate regex with a type check
nodes.why: The regex runs only for strings, so invalid types fail immediately.
nodes.alternative: Separate this into an early `if` return.
nodes.tradeoff: Compact one-liner, but a bit denser to read.

nodes.title: Block consecutive underscores with lookahead
nodes.why: This pattern guarantees `__` never appears anywhere in the username.
nodes.tradeoff: Powerful and concise, but lookaheads can be harder for some users to parse.

nodes.title: Require ending character to be alphanumeric
nodes.why: It prevents usernames from ending with `_`, matching the rule directly.
nodes.tradeoff: Keeps logic in one regex, but increases regex complexity.
